title: U.S. imposes sweeping advanced-computing and semiconductor controls on China
actors: U.S. Commerce/BIS; China; Nvidia; AMD; semiconductor toolmakers
rationale: Major structural escalation targeting advanced chips, semiconductor tools, supercomputing, and U.S.-person support.
notes: Duplicate of V2 event E005, BIS Advanced Semiconductor Export Controls.

title: U.S. tightens AI-chip export rules to China
actors: U.S. Commerce/BIS; China; Nvidia; AMD; Intel
rationale: Follow-on tightening to the 2022 controls, closing loopholes and extending restrictions to additional chips and entities.
notes: Strong candidate; verify final rule publication date and map to first trading day if needed.

title: U.S. expands semiconductor controls targeting China's advanced-chip capacity
actors: U.S. Commerce/BIS; China; semiconductor equipment firms; HBM supply chain
rationale: Further control expansion targeting advanced-chip capacity, chipmaking equipment, HBM, software, and Chinese entities.
notes: Strong candidate; code as export-control escalation and strategic-technology restriction.

title: Huawei added to U.S. Entity List
actors: U.S. Commerce/BIS; Huawei; U.S. technology suppliers
rationale: Early structural U.S.-China technology-decoupling event restricting Huawei's access to U.S. technology.
notes: Strong 2019 anchor event for tech-decoupling period.

title: U.S. foreign-direct-product rule tightened against Huawei
actors: U.S. Commerce/BIS; Huawei; TSMC; global foundries
rationale: Restricted Huawei's ability to source chips manufactured abroad using U.S. technology.
notes: High relevance to Taiwan foundry geopolitics; check same-day confounding with TSMC Arizona announcement.

title: SMIC added to U.S. Entity List
actors: U.S. Commerce/BIS; SMIC; China
rationale: Restricted China's leading foundry from accessing key U.S. technology.
notes: Strong candidate for China semiconductor capacity constraint and substitution narratives.

title: U.S. Japan and Netherlands align on China chip-tool controls
actors: United States; Japan; Netherlands; ASML; Tokyo Electron
rationale: Multilateral alignment broadened advanced semiconductor equipment restrictions beyond unilateral U.S. controls.
notes: Important allied-control event; verify announcement timing against market close.

title: Netherlands announces semiconductor equipment export licensing rules
actors: Dutch government; ASML; China
rationale: Dutch licensing rules affected exports of advanced semiconductor manufacturing equipment to China.
notes: Strong ASML and China fab-capex event.

title: Dutch restrictions halt some ASML China shipments
actors: Dutch government; ASML; China
rationale: Reported halt or license restriction affected shipment of certain advanced ASML systems to China.
notes: Need date reconciliation: rule effective date, license revocation, company disclosure, and trading-day timing may differ.

title: Taiwan adds Huawei and SMIC to export-control list
actors: Taiwan government; Huawei; SMIC
rationale: Taiwan tightened controls on leading Chinese semiconductor firms.
notes: Strong Taiwan-specific technology-security alignment event; verify official publication and first trading day.

title: China bars Micron products from critical information infrastructure
actors: Cyberspace Administration of China; Micron; Chinese critical infrastructure operators
rationale: China's cybersecurity review led to restrictions on Micron purchases by critical infrastructure operators.
notes: Strong countermeasure event; code as economic coercion or supply-chain security.

title: China announces gallium and germanium export controls
actors: China Ministry of Commerce; global semiconductor and materials buyers
rationale: Licensing controls covered materials used in chips, RF electronics, optics, and defense supply chains.
notes: Strong critical-materials leverage event.

title: China announces graphite export controls
actors: China Ministry of Commerce; battery and strategic-technology supply chains
rationale: Export controls targeted graphite, a key battery and strategic-technology input.
notes: Useful for broader strategic-materials coverage beyond semiconductors.

title: China moves to replace Intel and AMD chips in government systems
actors: Chinese government; Intel; AMD; domestic Chinese CPU suppliers
rationale: China approved domestic processors and moved toward replacing foreign hardware in government systems.
notes: Need evidence review for exact public-disclosure date and whether 2023-12-26 or later reporting is the market-relevant date.

title: China bans U.S. exports of gallium germanium antimony and related dual-use materials
actors: China Ministry of Commerce; United States; technology and defense supply chains
rationale: China restricted or banned exports to the United States of several strategic materials after U.S. semiconductor-control expansion.
notes: Strong retaliation and supply-chain-security event.

title: U.S. CHIPS and Science Act signed
actors: U.S. government; semiconductor manufacturers
rationale: Major U.S. industrial-policy package with subsidies and tax incentives for domestic semiconductor capacity.
notes: Strong state-support event; code as strategic importance and industrial policy.

title: TSMC announces $12 billion Arizona fab
actors: TSMC; U.S. federal and state officials
rationale: TSMC committed to an advanced U.S. fabrication facility.
notes: Relevant but same-day Huawei FDPR creates confounding risk; consider if event window can isolate.

title: TSMC expands Arizona plan to $40 billion and second fab
actors: TSMC; U.S. government; chip customers
rationale: TSMC expanded its Arizona footprint amid CHIPS Act implementation and supply-chain-security pressure.
notes: Duplicate of V2 event E006, TSMC Arizona US$40B Expansion.

title: European Chips Act published in EU Official Journal
actors: European Union; EU member states; semiconductor firms
rationale: EU framework aimed to strengthen the European semiconductor ecosystem and reduce supply dependence.
notes: Good allied industrial-policy comparator.

title: TSMC opens first Japan plant in Kumamoto
actors: TSMC; Japan; Sony; Denso; Toyota
rationale: TSMC opened a Japan fab with substantial Japanese government support and major local customers.
notes: Strong Taiwan-linked diversification event; code as strategic investment and state support.

title: U.S. announces preliminary CHIPS award for TSMC Arizona
actors: U.S. Commerce; TSMC
rationale: Preliminary CHIPS package included grants and loans for TSMC's Arizona expansion.
notes: Duplicate of V2 event E011, TSMC CHIPS Act Preliminary Terms.

title: China establishes Big Fund III
actors: China Integrated Circuit Industry Investment Fund; Chinese state-linked banks; Beijing
rationale: Large state-backed semiconductor fund signaled continued import-substitution and domestic-capacity investment.
notes: Strong China state-support counterpoint to U.S. and allied subsidy events.

title: U.S. House Speaker Nancy Pelosi visits Taiwan
actors: Nancy Pelosi; Taiwan government; China
rationale: High-profile U.S. congressional visit to Taiwan triggered Beijing's backlash.
notes: Duplicate of V2 event E002, Pelosi Visit.

title: China begins large-scale live-fire drills around Taiwan
actors: PLA; Taiwan; United States; Japan
rationale: PLA exercises encircled Taiwan after Pelosi's visit and included missile activity.
notes: Duplicate of V2 event E004, PLA Military Exercises After Pelosi Visit.

title: Lai Ching-te wins Taiwan presidential election
actors: Taiwan voters; DPP; Lai Ching-te; China
rationale: DPP retained the presidency despite Beijing's opposition to Lai's political positioning.
notes: Strong political-signaling event; weekend date requires trading-day alignment.

title: China launches Joint Sword-2024A after Lai inauguration
actors: PLA; Taiwan; Lai administration
rationale: Large PLA drills followed Lai's inauguration.
notes: Duplicate of V2 event E013, Joint Sword-2024A.

title: China launches Joint Sword drills after Tsai-McCarthy meeting
actors: PLA; Tsai Ing-wen; Kevin McCarthy; United States
rationale: PLA exercises followed Tsai's U.S. transit and meeting with the U.S. House Speaker.
notes: Duplicate of V2 event E007, Joint Sword 2023.

title: Nauru switches recognition from Taiwan to China
actors: Nauru; Taiwan; China
rationale: Nauru severed ties with Taiwan and recognized China shortly after Taiwan's election.
notes: Strong recognition-diplomacy event; already appears in broader V3 file but not conservative V2 baseline.

title: Honduras cuts ties with Taiwan and recognizes China
actors: Honduras; Taiwan; China
rationale: Honduras formally switched diplomatic recognition to Beijing.
notes: Strong recognition-diplomacy event; weekend date requires trading-day alignment.

title: U.S. TAIPEI Act signed
actors: United States; Taiwan; China; Taiwan's diplomatic partners
rationale: U.S. law supported Taiwan's international space and diplomatic relationships.
notes: Good legislative countermeasure to Beijing's diplomatic-isolation campaign.
